metadata.title: Pensions and LDI - the retirement balance sheet inside private markets, and the UK 2022 doom-loop precedent
metadata.frame: Pension funds are a multi-trillion retirement pool that has migrated ~a third of the two largest US funds into manager-marked illiquids and uses leveraged liability-driven investing (LDI) that already broke once - the UK September 2022 gilt doom-loop. This block documents the LDI precedent, the US public-pension alternatives shift, the denominator/self-marking problem, and the demographic drawdown. Companion to [[macro-uncovered-risk-pools]], [[macro-private-credit-marks]], and [[self_marked_value]].
metadata.relevance: Adds the pension pool to the uncovered stack; connects the self-marked-value thread to retirement balance sheets; supplies the LDI leverage precedent for the Fed-reaction and contagion threads; feeds the municipal block (pension crowd-out).
metadata.sources: Bank of England + SUERF/Parliament (UK LDI 2022); The Pensions Regulator (250bp buffer); Equable State of Pensions 2025; Pew (alts shift); CalPERS/CalSTRS investment disclosures; Milliman PFI (corporate DB); PBGC FY2024; Jefferies H1-2025 Secondary Market Review; S&P Global (PE over-allocation). Full URLs in the rendered .md.

uk_ldi_2022.summary: A leveraged hedge strategy turned a gilt sell-off into a self-reinforcing collateral spiral - the live precedent.

points.detail: The 23 Sep 2022 'mini-budget' spiked 30yr gilt yields ~120bp+ in days; leveraged LDI funds posted gilts as collateral, so falling gilt prices triggered margin calls -> forced gilt selling -> higher yields -> more calls (the Bank of England's 'vicious spiral').

UK DB liabilities hedged via LDI grew ~£400bn (2011) to ~£1.5T (2020) - roughly two-thirds of UK GDP. The BoE announced gilt buying up to £5bn/day (the '£65bn' headline = the 13-day ceiling) but ACTUALLY bought ~£19.3bn - the backstop worked mostly via announcement.

BoE staff estimate margin/collateral calls >£70bn and forced LDI gilt sales >£36bn over 23 Sep-14 Oct 2022. Aftermath: TPR/FPC require leveraged LDI to hold a >=250bp yield-resilience buffer plus an operational buffer. A 2025 gilt sell-off re-tested it - wider buffers held without BoE intervention.

us_public_pension_alts.summary: Roughly a third of the two largest US funds now sits in valuation-priced illiquids to chase a ~6.9% assumption.

points.detail: US state/local plans: aggregate funded ratio ~80% (2024) improving to ~82.5% (2025); unfunded ~$1.27T (2025, down from $1.54T). FY2025 returns averaged ~9.5% vs an assumed ~6.9% - the improvement is return-driven, not structural, so it reverses in a drawdown.
Alts rose from ~11% (2006) to ~26% (2016) to ~34% (2022) of public-pension portfolios as public equity fell below 50% - plans took illiquidity/asset risk to hit the assumed return.

CalPERS (~$556B, Jun 30 2025): private equity ~17.7%, real assets ~13.1%, private debt ~3.8% (new private-credit sleeve target ~8%) - ~34% illiquid. CalSTRS (~$370B): private equity 15.1%, real estate 12.8% - ~28% illiquid. Roughly a third of each sits in marks, not prices.

denominator_problem.summary: Pension books carry manager-marked NAVs - the same self-marked defect, on the retirement balance sheet.

points.detail: Private-market values in pension books are manager/valuation-priced, not market-priced - directly the self-marked-value theme ([[self_marked_value]], [[macro-private-credit-marks]]). Stale private marks lag public drawdowns, pushing funds OVER their PE targets: >50% of US plans were over-allocated to PE in 2025 filings (S&P Global).

H1-2025 secondaries: average LP portfolios traded ~90% of NAV, buyout ~94%, tail-end (>10yr) funds below 75%, distressed sellers 25%+ discounts (Jefferies) - direct evidence the NAV marks overstate realizable value.

corporate_and_drawdown.summary: Corporate DB is overfunded (and offloading to insurers); public DB is in net outflow.

points.detail: Milliman 100 funded ratio ~108% (end-2025) - corporate plans are OVERfunded, driving LDI de-risking and pension risk transfer (annuity buyouts that relocate liabilities onto life insurers, [[spec-insurance-bermuda]]). Do not conflate with underfunded public plans (~82%).

Public plans run net cash outflow ~1.7% of assets (2024); mature plans (retirees > active contributors) rely on returns/asset sales to pay benefits. Negative cash flow + illiquid alts means stress can force sales of the LIQUID sleeve (or secondaries at a discount), since privates can't be sold at NAV.

synthesis.summary: Leverage that already broke once, plus self-marked illiquids on the retirement balance sheet.

points.detail: Pensions are where leveraged rate-hedging (LDI, proven fragile in 2022) and manager-marked illiquids (the self-marked-value defect) meet the household's retirement claim. The same rate/credit common factor ([[macro-cross-sectional-analysis]], ~91%) that prices the bank HTM hole and private-credit NAVs also prices pension alts and gilt/Treasury LDI collateral - and the demographic outflow removes the buy-and-hold cushion.

non_claims: No claim US public pensions are insolvent - aggregate funded ratio ~82%; the point is leverage (LDI) and self-marked illiquids plus net outflow, not present insolvency.
The UK '£65bn' is the BoE ceiling; actual purchases were ~£19.3bn - the distinction is stated, not the headline.
Corporate DB (~108%) and public DB (~82%) cut opposite directions and are not conflated.
Forced-selling-in-stress is graded weak (analytical), not a realized loss event.
Overlay edges are EXCLUDED from the SCC / Z3 / TLA+ proofs.

edges.raw_instrument: direct-lending allocations (CalPERS ~8% target, CalSTRS adding ~2%) - the fastest-growing pension sleeve, manager-marked NAVs
edges.note: OVERLAY: self-marked allocation; excluded from proofs

edges.raw_instrument: LP commitments to manager-marked PE/real-asset interests (~34% of CalPERS, ~28% of CalSTRS illiquid; >50% of US plans over PE target via the denominator effect)
leveraged gilt collateral (~£1.5T hedged) - the 2022 doom loop forced >£36bn of gilt selling on margin calls; 250bp+ buffer reform; same sovereign-collateral fragility as US Treasuries
edges.note: OVERLAY: LDI collateral spiral; excluded from proofs

edges.raw_instrument: pension risk transfer / annuity buyouts (corporate LDI de-risking relocates liabilities onto life insurers and their captive marks)
edges.note: OVERLAY: liability relocation; excluded from proofs

limits: Documents the UK Sep-2022 LDI doom loop (~£1.5T hedged; calls >£70bn; forced sales >£36bn; BoE ceiling £65bn / actual ~£19.3bn; 250bp buffer reform); US public-pension alts shift (~5% 2000 -> ~35% 2022; CalPERS ~34% / CalSTRS ~28% illiquid; unfunded ~$1.27T; ~82% funded); the manager-marked NAV/denominator problem (>50% over PE target; secondaries 75-94% of NAV); corporate DB overfunded (~108%) offloading to insurers; and net-outflow demographic drawdown. Overlay edges excluded from proofs.

checks.result: ~£1.5T hedged (2020); calls >£70bn, forced sales >£36bn (BoE/SUERF); BoE bought ~£19.3bn of a £65bn ceiling; 250bp buffer (TPR Apr 2023).
>50% of plans over PE target (S&P 2025); secondaries avg ~90% of NAV, tail <75% (Jefferies H1-2025).
Milliman 100 ~108% (end-2025); pension risk transfer to insurers; public plans net outflow ~1.7%/yr.
